Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Jun-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Hoyt Gibson and Brenda Gibson
Paul Lannus, Esq. of LANKFORD & CRAWFORD LLP (925-300-3520), appearing for defendant / opposing party Ford Motor Company

RULING:
Plaintiffs HOYT GIBSON and BRENDA GIBSON's motion to strike or tax costs. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion is denied. Defendant Ford Motor Company is awarded the full amount of its claimed costs of $1.012 for photocopying of exhibits. While there is a conflict in appellate authority whether cost for copying of exhibits that are not actually used at trial are recoverable, the better reasoned decisions hold that such costs are recoverable where, as here, they were reasonably incurred for trial . (E.g. Benach v. County of Los Angeles (2007) 149 Cal. App. 4th 836, 855-57; Applegate v. St. Francis Lutheran Church (1994) 23 Cal. App. 4th 361, 364). The foregoing is in exercise of the court's discretion under CCP 1033.5(c)(4). Order and judgment signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
